compiled_state.title: As a platform engineer, I can follow root INSTALL.md step-by-step to install DSO from the Claude Code marketplace, install required and optional dependencies on macOS using homebrew, and understand how to start onboarding
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new root INSTALL.md file with step-by-step marketplace installation instructions, required and optional dependency lists with macOS homebrew steps, and a /dso:onboarding orientation section.
**Why**: The current installation documentation at plugins/dso/docs/INSTALL.md is written for the git-clone path and buried inside the plugin directory. Platform engineers need a clean, root-level document using the current marketplace-based installation path.
**Scope**:
- IN: Create root INSTALL.md; marketplace install commands (/plugin marketplace add and /plugin install); required deps (bash >=4.0, GNU coreutils as blocking prereqs); optional deps (ast-grep); macOS homebrew installation steps; link to brew.sh; /dso:onboarding description with time expectations; link to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md using correct relative path
- OUT: Integration setup section (Story D); removing old INSTALL.md (Story F); updating CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (Story E)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, root INSTALL.md provides step-by-step marketplace installation instructions using `/plugin marketplace add navapbc/digital-service-orchestra` followed by `/plugin install dso@digital-service-orchestra`
  <- Satisfies: "Root INSTALL.md provides step-by-step marketplace installation instructions"
- When this story is complete, root INSTALL.md lists required and optional dependencies separately with macOS homebrew installation steps and a link to the official homebrew install guide at brew.sh
  <- Satisfies: "Required and optional dependencies listed separately, macOS homebrew steps, brew.sh link"
- When this story is complete, root INSTALL.md includes bash >= 4.0 and GNU coreutils as blocking prerequisites appearing before the /dso:onboarding step, with homebrew install commands for each
  <- Satisfies: scenario analysis finding (macOS out-of-the-box silently fails without these)
- When this story is complete, INSTALL.md includes a description of /dso:onboarding that sets realistic time expectations and contains a link to the configuration reference
  <- Satisfies: "INSTALL.md includes a description of /dso:onboarding with realistic time expectations and a link to the configuration reference"
- When this story is complete, all links from root INSTALL.md to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md use the correct relative path (plugins/dso/docs/CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md)
  <- Satisfies: adversarial review finding -- relative links from same-directory INSTALL.md would break when file moves to root

## Considerations

- [Reliability] bash >= 4.0 and GNU coreutils must appear as BLOCKING prerequisites before the /dso:onboarding step -- macOS ships with bash 3.2 and BSD coreutils, which silently fail with DSO scripts
- [Integration] Verified: marketplace install commands are `/plugin marketplace add navapbc/digital-service-orchestra` and `/plugin install dso@digital-service-orchestra` (confirmed in epic research)
- [Maintainability] The existing root README.md contains an ast-grep optional dependency section. Ensure this content is either migrated to INSTALL.md's optional dependencies section or explicitly marked as superseded before Story B rewrites README.md

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue -- one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Story complete: all 3 child tasks closed, completion-verifier PASS on all 5 done definitions.
comments.author: Test
